title: Craspedia glabrata
taxonomy: Craspedia glabrata is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family Asteraceae. It is native to Australia, where it grows in dry to moist environments. This plant is classified as a member of the genus Craspedia, which includes several other species of daisies. The classification of Craspedia glabrata is further refined to the species level based on its unique characteristics and genetic makeup. As a member of the Asteraceae family, it is closely related to other daisy species such as the Gerbera daisy.

intro: Craspedia glabrata, commonly known as yellow buttons or woolly button, is a small annual plant native to Australia, characterized by its bright yellow, globular flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including fever reduction, cough relief, and inflammation due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Craspedia glabrata include flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Craspedia glabrata include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, often used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma.
